medications.indication_rationale: First-line vasodilator to reduce frequency/severity of Raynaud's attacks in systemic sclerosis; commenced/titrated by the GP after review (not initiated acutely without BP assessment).
For the ischaemic pain during rewarming.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult, no contraindication stated)

medications.indication_rationale: Beta-blockers can worsen Raynaud's vasospasm and should be avoided.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Warm the whole body (layers, warm room, warm - not hot - water on the wrists/hands, warm drink) and watch the finger colour; keep the house warmer and wear gloves before cold exposure.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Core rewarming reverses the vasospasm and prevents recurrence.

patient_education_points.point: This is a severe Raynaud's attack — the blood vessels in your fingers clamp shut with cold. Because both hands are affected and the colour is coming back as you warm, this is spasm, not a blocked artery, so your fingers are not about to be lost.

Warm your whole body now (not just your hands) — layers, a warm room, warm (not hot) water, and a warm drink; take paracetamol for the pain. Keep the house warmer and wear gloves before going out in the cold.
Go to ED or call 000 if any finger stays completely white or blue and numb for more than an hour or two despite warming, or if a fingertip turns black or develops an open sore.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to rewarming, commence/titrate a vasodilator (nifedipine), assess digital ischaemia risk and CREST management.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: A digit fixed white/blue/numb despite warming, or turning black/ulcerating — ED now.

must_include_items: Guide immediate CORE rewarming and reassess colour/pain
Recognise a bilateral, recovering attack as severe Raynaud's, not fixed ischaemia
Provide a clear digital-ischaemia safety-net (ED if a digit stays white/blue/numb despite warming or turns black)
Arrange urgent GP review for a vasodilator (avoid beta-blockers)
acceptable_alternatives: Direct ED if warming fails or fixed ischaemia is suspected
errors_of_commission: Reassuring 'just Raynaud's' without a rewarming trial or safety-net
Reflex ambulance for a reversing bilateral attack
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: High-risk case: passing requires core rewarming guidance PLUS a digital-ischaemia safety-net. Missing fixed ischaemia (no safety-net) or over-triaging a reversing attack are failures.
